title: GET vs POST
description: Understand the difference between GET and POST methods.

slides.title: Two Common Methods
slides.content:
# GET and POST

*   **GET**: Appends data to the URL (query string). Data is visible, has length limits, and can be bookmarked. Used for retrieving data (search forms, links).
*   **POST**: Sends data in the HTTP request body. Data is not visible in URL, no practical size limit, and cannot be bookmarked. Used for sending sensitive or large data (login, file uploads).

Never use GET for passwords or sensitive information.

slides.title: GET Example
slides.content:
```html
<form action="search.php" method="get">
    <input type="text" name="query">
    <input type="submit">
</form>
```
When submitted, the browser goes to `search.php?query=something`. In PHP, you access via `$_GET['query']`.

slides.title: POST Example
slides.content:
```html
<form action="login.php" method="post">
    <input type="text" name="username">
    <input type="password" name="password">
    <input type="submit">
</form>
```
Data is sent in the request body, not visible in URL. Accessed via `$_POST['username']`.

quizData.question: Which method should you use for a login form?
quizData.explanation: POST is preferred for sensitive data because it doesn't appear in the URL.
